# E-commerce Product Image Workflow

> Open-source, non-technical-friendly workflow for transforming supplier product photos into publish-ready e-commerce assets (showcase / spec / how-to images) while enforcing: product identity consistency, visual differentiation across SKUs, English-only output text, deterministic template-rendered spec text, and one-vote auditable QA rejection criteria. Workflow + templates + prompts repo — not a model, not a service.

This repo is **a method**, not a tool that runs. It's targeted at e-commerce operations teams, content ops, creative production vendors, and automation builders who want a *reproducible* image pipeline that produces consistent results across SKUs and across operators, regardless of which underlying AI image model they choose (Midjourney, Flux, SDXL, Imagen, etc.).

## Key docs

- [README.md](README.md): English overview (this file's repo root).
- [README.zh-CN.md](README.zh-CN.md): Chinese version.
- [docs/en/quickstart.md](docs/en/quickstart.md): English quick start.
- [docs/zh-CN/quickstart.md](docs/zh-CN/quickstart.md): Chinese quick start.
- [docs/en/](docs/en/): English documentation set.
- [docs/zh-CN/](docs/zh-CN/): Chinese documentation set.
- Docs site (GitHub Pages): https://tytsxai.github.io/ecommerce-product-image-workflow/
- [templates/](templates/): Manager input form, QA checklist, batch record, style packs.
- [prompts/](prompts/): Reusable prompt templates per image type.
- [examples/](examples/): Reference examples.

## What this workflow provides

- **Manager input form** (CSV): captures product info, target scene, brand voice, deliverable count per SKU
- **Style packs** (JSON): reusable background / mood / palette presets that operators select from rather than re-describe
- **Prompt templates**: per image type (showcase / spec / how-to), parameterized over the manager input + style pack
- **QA checklist** (CSV): one-vote rejection rules for product identity drift, text-language violations, scene-mismatch, font-licensing issues
- **Batch record** (CSV): per-batch audit log so a result can always be traced back to inputs
- **Three image types** with explicit definitions:
  1. **Showcase** — hero / lifestyle imagery
  2. **Spec** — dimension / specification text cards
  3. **How-to** — instructional visual cards
- **English-only output text** rule, with deterministic template rendering for spec/how-to text

## What this workflow does NOT do

- ❌ Provide model weights or hosted image generation — bring your own (Midjourney / SDXL / Flux / Imagen / DALL-E)
- ❌ Run automation — provides specs that can plug into n8n / Make / Zapier
- ❌ Give legal advice on platform policy / commercial-use rights of generated images
- ❌ Provide font licenses or commercial assets — the workflow says "use commercially licensed assets", but doesn't ship them
- ❌ Handle video / 3D / AR assets — Phase 1 is image-only by design

## Common questions

- **Which image model should I use?** The workflow is model-agnostic; it works with Midjourney, Flux dev/schnell, SDXL, Imagen, DALL-E, etc. Pick based on your platform's commercial-use terms and quality fit.
- **Is the output commercially usable?** Depends on the model's license and your jurisdiction. The workflow enforces commercial-only fonts/assets in its QA rules, but you must still verify the AI model output license yourself.
- **How is this different from Canva templates / Photoshop actions?** Those are *tools*. This is a *workflow specification*: it tells you what artifacts must exist, what each one must contain, and what gates content must pass before publish. The actual generation can happen in any tool.
- **Can I plug it into n8n / Make / Zapier?** Yes — that's the explicit Phase 2 goal. The CSV inputs and prompt templates are designed to be machine-readable.
- **Non-technical team — can they actually use this?** Yes — that's the design constraint. Managers fill a CSV, operators run prompts, QA uses a checklist. No code needed.

## Repository

- GitHub: https://github.com/tytsxai/ecommerce-product-image-workflow
- License: MIT
- Type: Workflow specification + templates + prompts repo (no executable code)
- Phase: 1 (image assets); Phase 2 (automation adapters) and Phase 3 (video) on roadmap
- Author: tytsxai
